assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); classified as regressions against baseline. Overall eval pass rate: 96% (54/56). Repetitive Test Detection: N/A. Test Documentation: N/A.' The Test Quality verdict is WARN, driven by the Eval Quality WARN component.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 35: 'Eval Result Review detected: Review ID 40001 from github-actions[bot] (matched all 3 detection criteria: author is github-actions[bot], body contains "## Eval Results", body contains "sdlc-workflow/run-evals").' The Eval Quality Details section (lines 37-50) contains extracted eval metrics including per-eval pass rates, failing assertion details, and baseline comparison.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies comment 50001 from reviewer-b as a 'code change request' with reasoning about the reviewer's directive language and CHANGES_REQUESTED review state. No mention of eval detection, eval metrics, or eval-related processing anywhere in the file. In report.md, comment 50001 appears under 'Review Feedback Summary' (line 30) as a normal review comment, separate from the Eval Quality Details section.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row: 'WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate)'. Lines 47-48 detail the failing assertions: '1. Convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) -- the classification output does not document CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis' and '2. No sub-task created for review comment 30002 -- classified as suggestion with no convention upgrade attempted'.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-eval-3.md exists and its description (line 3) states: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures in the verify-pr eval suite. Two assertions are failing at 85% pass rate (11/13 passed, 2 failed).' The file includes the failing assertion text and evidence in the Review Context section (lines 28-35), quoting both assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation with their evidence.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-eval-3.md lines 28-35 contain '## Review Context' with two quoted assertion blocks: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002...' with evidence 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented' and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-eval-3.md lines 38-39: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. The exact URL matches the expected value.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval-3 regression failures investigated; root-cause task warranted for implement-task convention upgrade handling.' The verdict is 'DONE', not N/A, confirming the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are analysis/reporting artifacts: report.md (verification report), criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations), review-50001.md (review comment classification), subtask-eval-3.md (sub-task description). No code files (*.ts, *.js, *.py, *.md in plugin source directories) were modified. The outputs directory contains only verification and sub-task description files.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md: Scope Containment (line 7) evaluates 'PR modifies style-conventions.md and SKILL.md, matching the task's Files to Modify exactly. No out-of-scope files, no unimplemented files.' Sensitive Patterns (line 10) reports 'No sensitive patterns detected in added lines. The diff contains only Markdown documentation content.' Acceptance Criteria (line 12) reports '7 of 7 criteria met.' Each criterion file (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md) evaluates its criterion against the diff content with specific quotes from the diff, with no references to eval results or eval processing.
